subject: Astronomy & Space
title: Earth: The Goldilocks Planet
description: A quick, practical guide to Earth: The Goldilocks Planet.

hook.text: Earth sits in the habitable zone—not too hot, not too cold. Liquid water flows on the surface.

buildup.text: A magnetic field shields us from the solar wind. Plate tectonics recycle carbon and regulate climate.

discovery.text: The Moon stabilizes Earth's tilt, keeping seasons mild enough for complex life to evolve.

twist.text: Earth is the only known place where water exists as solid, liquid, and gas simultaneously.

climax.text: From space, the thin blue atmosphere looks fragile. Carl Sagan called it a 'pale blue dot.'

punchline.text: Just right for water. Just right for life. A cosmic fluke.

quiz.question: How does the Moon help make Earth habitable?
options: It stabilizes Earth's axial tilt, keeping seasonal changes mild
It blocks harmful asteroids from reaching Earth
It generates tidal energy that warms the oceans
